```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
mu0   <- 5
mu1   <- sample(c(6, 7, 8), 1)
sigma <- sample(c(2, 3, 4), 1)
n     <- sample(c(25, 36, 49, 64), 1)
alpha <- 0.05

z_crit  <- 1.645
c_crit  <- mu0 + z_crit * sigma / sqrt(n)
beta_val <- round(pnorm((c_crit - mu1) / (sigma / sqrt(n))), 4)

## Respostas
correct <- beta_val
w1 <- round(1 - beta_val, 4)
w2 <- round(pnorm((mu1 - c_crit) / (sigma / sqrt(n))), 4)
w3 <- round(pnorm((c_crit - mu0) / (sigma / sqrt(n))), 4)
w4 <- round(0.05, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $H_0:\mu = `r mu0`$ vs. $H_1:\mu > `r mu0`$ com $\sigma = `r sigma`$, $n = `r n`$ e $\alpha = 0{,}05$: a regiao critica e $\bar{X} > `r round(c_crit,4)`$. Se o verdadeiro valor e $\mu = `r mu1`$, a probabilidade do Erro Tipo II e $P(\bar{X} \leq `r round(c_crit,4)` \mid \mu = `r mu1`)$:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
$P(\text{Erro Tipo II}) = P\!\left(Z \leq \frac{`r round(c_crit,4)` - `r mu1`}{`r sigma`/\sqrt{`r n`}}\right) = \Phi\!\left(`r round((c_crit - mu1)/(sigma/sqrt(n)),4)`\right) = `r beta_val`$.

O poder correspondente seria $1 - \beta = `r round(1-beta_val,4)`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S1Q9_prob_erro_tipo_II
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
